_note: Hand-written MOCK catalog-judge output for the NEAR-DUP BOTH case. A MATCHING transcript: recognizes the dedup near-dup flag as a FALSE POSITIVE (the two resources are engine-specific and mutually exclusive) and emits the EXPECTED verdict 'both' — no uid wins or loses. The verdict-matcher MUST score this as a calibration PASS.
rationale: The dedup stage flagged these as a near-dup on a 0.91 similarity score driven by shared migration boilerplate. On the merits they are genuinely DISTINCT: A is Postgres-specific (CREATE INDEX CONCURRENTLY, transactional DDL rollback) and B is MySQL-specific (online DDL, explicitly NO transactional DDL — forward-only compensation). Neither subsumes the other; a project on one engine needs exactly one of them. The near-dup flag was a FALSE POSITIVE — verdict BOTH, admit both, no winner/loser. Both security + eval signals GREEN.
